title: INIT and INIT_ONE macros: unchecked PyArray_EMPTY — NULL dereference in ~46 functions
author: devdanzin
body:
The `INIT` macro (move functions) and `INIT_ONE` macro (reduce functions) call `PyArray_EMPTY` and immediately use the result without a NULL check. If `PyArray_EMPTY` fails (OOM), the subsequent `init_iter2` / `PyArray_DATA` dereferences NULL.

Because these are macros expanded by the template system, a single missing NULL check propagates to ~20 move functions (via `INIT`) and ~26 reduce functions (via `INIT_ONE`) — approximately 46 crash sites total.

**File(s):** `move_template.c:21-24` (INIT), `reduce_template.c:26-32` (INIT_ONE)

**Suggested fix:** Add a NULL check in each macro:

```c
// INIT — before:
#define INIT(dtype) \\
    PyObject *y = PyArray_EMPTY(PyArray_NDIM(a), PyArray_SHAPE(a), dtype, 0); \\
    iter2 it; \\
    init_iter2(&it, a, y, axis);

// INIT — after:
#define INIT(dtype) \\
    PyObject *y = PyArray_EMPTY(PyArray_NDIM(a), PyArray_SHAPE(a), dtype, 0); \\
    if (y == NULL) return NULL; \\
    iter2 it; \\
    init_iter2(&it, a, y, axis);
```

Same pattern for `INIT_ONE`:
```c
// INIT_ONE — add after PyArray_EMPTY:
    if (y == NULL) return NULL; \\
```

One-line fix per macro, ~46 crash sites protected.

See #518 for the complete report.

Found using [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit).
